clusters.clusterRisk: Medium. All three merge-301 targets are older (2024-vintage) pages with meaningful impression histories (2,136 / 1,634 / 673 imp), so external backlinks cannot be ruled out without a backlink tool — a manual backlink check on all three dying URLs is required before executing any 301, and equity-bearing pages should still 301 to the survivor (not be deleted). The 301s are GATED and must not be executed from this manifest. Residual cannibalization risk after consolidation is low but nonzero: the differentiated TIA page still shares the 'tenant improvement' token with the survivor, so its H2s must be rewritten to the lease-finance/allowance angle before it is considered clean, otherwise it will re-compete on 'commercial tenant improvement'. Consolidation is directionally strong because the survivor already outranks every merged page on the shared head terms, so folding equity inward should raise, not risk, the survivor's position. Monitor combined clicks at 6 weeks; the per-redirect rollback (remove 301 + restore from export within 30 days if survivor loses >20% of combined clicks) is the safety net.
